pageTitle: PowerPoint to PDF Converter — Free Online | SmallPDF.us
pageDescription: Convert PowerPoint to PDF in seconds. Free, no signup. Every slide transfers perfectly — fonts, images, layouts all intact. Works on any browser.
heroSubtitle: Turn your PowerPoint presentations into polished, shareable PDFs — no software needed, no account required.

stepsTitle: How to convert PPT to PDF
stepsSubtitle: Three steps. No installs, no fuss. Done in under a minute.
step1Desc: Drop your PPT or PPTX file onto the page, or click to open the file browser. Batch uploads work too — up to 10 files at once.
step2Desc: Our engine processes your slides automatically, preserving your layouts, fonts, images, and animations as closely as possible in the PDF output.
step3Desc: Your finished PDF is ready in moments. Download it to your device and it's ready to share, print, or archive — no extra steps.
featuresTitle: Built for real people, not just power users
featuresSubtitle: A clean, fast converter that just works — every time.
feature1Desc: Every slide in your presentation becomes a page in the PDF, in the exact same order. Nothing gets dropped or rearranged.
feature2Desc: Photos, charts, and graphics from your slides are embedded at full quality in the PDF. No blurry images, no missing visuals.
feature3Desc: Your text looks the same in the PDF as it does in PowerPoint. Font styles, weights, and sizes are all faithfully reproduced.
feature4Desc: Even multi-slide decks convert in under a minute. No waiting around while your files sit in a queue.
feature5Desc: Files are processed securely over encrypted connections and automatically deleted after conversion. We never store your presentations.
feature6Desc: No app to install, no plugin required. Open your browser on any device — laptop, tablet, or phone — and convert.
formatsSubtitle: We support all the major PowerPoint and presentation formats, so whatever version you're working with, you're covered.
formatItem1: PPTX — Microsoft PowerPoint (2007 and later), the most common format
formatItem2: PPT — Legacy PowerPoint files from older versions of Office
formatItem3: ODP — OpenDocument Presentation, used by LibreOffice and OpenOffice
formatItem4: Files up to 100 MB per upload
formatItem5: Batch conversion — up to 10 files at once
formatItem6: No software installation needed — runs entirely in your browser
pipelineCompat: Output is compatible with all PDF readers: Adobe, Chrome, Preview, and more
faqSubtitle: Everything you might want to know before hitting convert.
faq1Question: Is this PPT to PDF converter really free?
faq1Answer: Yes, completely free. No account needed, no credit card, no hidden trial. Upload your file, convert it, download your PDF — that's it.
faq2Question: Will my slides look the same in the PDF?
faq2Answer: In most cases, yes. Layouts, images, and text formatting are preserved faithfully. Slide animations and transitions won't carry over (since PDFs are static), but the visual content of each slide will look as expected.
faq3Question: What happens to my files after conversion?
faq3Answer: Your files are automatically deleted from our servers once the conversion is done. We process everything over encrypted connections and never store, view, or share your presentations.
faq4Question: Can I convert multiple PowerPoint files at once?
faq4Answer: Yes. You can upload up to 10 files at a time, each up to 100 MB. Each file is converted to its own PDF, which you can then download individually or as a zip.
faq5Question: What if my PDF looks different from the original?
faq5Answer: Slight differences can happen if your presentation uses custom fonts not embedded in the file, or certain advanced effects. For best results, embed your fonts before exporting from PowerPoint.
faq6Question: Can I open the converted PDF on my phone or tablet?
faq6Answer: Absolutely. The output is a standard PDF file that opens in any PDF reader — Adobe Acrobat, your browser, Apple Books, Google Drive, and so on.

convertingWait: Please wait while we convert your presentation. This usually takes under a minute.
security4: No data stored on our servers
eduPara1: PowerPoint files are great for building and editing presentations, but they come with a catch — they don't always look the same on every device. Fonts might be missing, layouts can shift, and without PowerPoint installed, some people can't open them at all. Converting to PDF solves all of that instantly.
eduWhyTitle: Top reasons people make the switch
eduReason1Desc: PDFs open on every device and operating system without needing PowerPoint or any special software.
eduReason2Desc: Your fonts, spacing, and slide design look identical whether the PDF is opened on Windows, Mac, iOS, or Android.
eduReason3Desc: PDFs are often significantly smaller than the original PPTX, making them faster to email and easier to upload.
eduReason4Desc: When you share a PDF, recipients can view it but not accidentally modify your content or formatting.
